title: Category Language Capture

content.statement: Whoever defines the language defines the market.
content.question: What's the phrase you want your customers to use to describe what you do, instead of a generic label a competitor could also claim?
content.equation: CLC = (Search Share + Verbatim Match + Sales Adoption) ÷ 3
sop: Write the generic category label people currently use for what you do
Write your own specific phrase for it instead
Use your phrase everywhere, on your site, in captions, in conversation, until it feels natural
Check quarterly whether customers are starting to use your phrase unprompted
content.explainer: If you own the vocabulary of the problem, every conversation in the market reinforces your category leadership.
questions: What's the phrase you want your customers to use to describe what you do, instead of a generic label a competitor could also claim?
When a customer describes you to a friend, do they use your words or a generic category label?
What word or phrase, if you owned it, would make every competitor's marketing sound like a copy of yours?
content.equation_example: Search Share 30%, Verbatim Match 45%, Sales Adoption 20%, averaged = 31.7% out of 100%. Higher means your phrase has taken over the category conversation. Lower means the generic label is still winning.
content.closing_statement: Own the words, own the category.

caption:
Principle Nº 4: Category Language Capture

Whoever defines the language defines the market.

Ask yourself: What's the phrase you want your customers to use to describe what you do, instead of a generic label a competitor could also claim?
